metadata.title: Cost-of-virtue: generosity from own resources
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft
metadata.design_intent: Companion to cov-allocation-001. The first probe tests fairness-with-found-money (returning an overpayment that was never yours). This one tests generosity-from-own-resources (writing a meaningful check to a cause you care about, from money that IS yours). Both are inverted probes — the ladder asks at what stake the user does the ethical action. The pair covers the two distinct dimensions of resource-allocation morality: not taking-what-isn't-yours and actively giving-what-is.
metadata.domain_anchor: Aknin et al. 2013/2022 prosocial-spending literature (cross-culturally robust positive-affect finding from real giving); Andreoni 1989/1990 warm-glow and pure-altruism distinction. Empirical anchor for stake-ladder calibration: typical effective-altruism community donor profile data (GiveWell, EA Forum) shows median annual donations cluster well below $10K for most income brackets.

framing_prompt: A cause you genuinely care about is at a critical funding moment. A one-time substantial donation from you would meaningfully shift the outcome — there's a specific, concrete impact you'd be enabling. The money comes from your own discretionary savings: you can afford it, but it's not nothing. No matching grant; no tax leverage; no public recognition.
framing_question: What's the smallest donation at which you'd actually write the check?

no_option.text: I wouldn't make a donation of any of these amounts to this cause.

alternate_no_option.text: I'd write a check at any of these amounts (the lowest is well within what I'd give to a cause I cared about).

analysis.no_break_point_handling: Code 'never' as Inf; code 'always_give_token' as 0. Code as numeric for the analyzer. Note that the cost direction is OPPOSITE to cov-truth-001: lower break-point = more generous (the ethical pole). Inverted-probe convention; analyzer reads from analysis block.
analysis.interpretation_note: Pair-with-cov-allocation-001 (fairness-with-found-money) is the headline within-domain analysis. The two probes cover orthogonal sides of resource-allocation morality. Note that the 'no public recognition' clause in the framing is load-bearing — without it, the probe measures signaling preference, not internal generosity-under-stake. Compare to qf-allocation-003-i05 (named vs anonymous donation) for the signaling-preference signal directly. Also pair with cov-truth-001: a user with low truth-probe (will lie for money) and low generosity-probe (will give large) is an inconsistent profile; users tend to be either prosocial-and-honest or self-interested-and-strategic. The off-diagonal cases are interesting.
analysis.longitudinal_signal: Within-user trajectory of log10(break_point + 100) reveals shifts in generosity disposition. Real-life giving events outside the app are the natural moderators.
